FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class BoxEl

Namespace
FluentGpu.Dsl
Assembly
FluentGpu.Dsl.dll

A box: container/layout node and/or a filled, optionally-clickable surface (VStack/HStack/Button/Box).

public sealed record BoxEl : Element, IEquatable<Element>, IEquatable<BoxEl>

object
Element
BoxEl

Properties

Accelerator

Keyboard-accelerator chord (WinUI KeyboardAccelerator): invokes OnClick from anywhere once focused routing leaves the chord unhandled (e.g. Ctrl+W close-tab).

public KeyAccelerator? Accelerator { get; init; }

Property Value

KeyAccelerator?

AccessKey

Access-key mnemonic (WinUI AccessKey): Alt+letter invokes OnClick. Uppercase 'A'..'Z'/'0'..'9'.

public char AccessKey { get; init; }

Property Value

char

Acrylic 

public AcrylicSpec? Acrylic { get; init; }

Property Value

AcrylicSpec?

ActivateOnEnter

WinUI KeyPress::Button bAcceptsReturn: false = Enter does NOT activate this clickable (it falls through to normal key routing) — CheckBox (CheckBox_Partial.cpp:27), RadioButton (RadioButton_Partial.cpp:30) and ToggleSwitch (ToggleSwitch_Partial.cpp:1002-1007) toggle on Space only. Space activation is unaffected.

public bool ActivateOnEnter { get; init; }

Property Value

bool

AlignItems 

public FlexAlign AlignItems { get; init; }

Property Value

FlexAlign

AlignSelf 

public FlexAlign AlignSelf { get; init; }

Property Value

FlexAlign

AllowFocusOnInteraction

WinUI AllowFocusOnInteraction: false = a pointer press never moves focus to this focusable (and never falls past it to an ancestor — focus stays where it was, AppBarButton_themeresources.xaml:136); keyboard Tab still reaches it. True (default) = press focuses the nearest focusable self-or-ancestor.

public bool AllowFocusOnInteraction { get; init; }

Property Value

bool

Animate

Opt this box into general layout-change animation: the host diffs its presented rect vs its new laid-out rect each commit and drives the residual through the spec's channels/dynamics (no relayout, no per-frame re-render). Null ⇒ snap (the default). See LayoutTransition.

public LayoutTransition? Animate { get; init; }

Property Value

LayoutTransition?

public ArcSpec? Arc { get; init; }

ArcSpec?

Basis

BorderBrush 

public GradientSpec? BorderBrush { get; init; }

Property Value

GradientSpec?

BorderColor 

public ColorF BorderColor { get; init; }

Property Value

ColorF

BorderWidth 

public float BorderWidth { get; init; }

Property Value

float

BrushTransitionMs

Implicit brush transition (WinUI BrushTransition): when a re-render changes Fill/BorderColor on this LIVE node (a logical state flip — checked, selected…), the displayed color cross-fades over this duration instead of snapping. NaN = snap (the default); WinUI control templates use 83ms.

public float BrushTransitionMs { get; init; }

Property Value

float

CanDrag

Marks this box a drag-reorder source (WinUI CanDragItems/CanReorderItems item container): a left press on it (or any non-draggable descendant) arms the engine's DragController; pointer travel past the 4px drag box (per-axis, ListViewBaseItem_Partial.cpp:1864-1878) promotes the press to a drag — the node follows the pointer at WinUI ListViewItemDragThemeOpacity 0.80 (ListViewItem_themeresources.xaml:7) with a lifted shadow, stops hit-testing, and the eventual release SUPPRESSES the click.

public bool CanDrag { get; init; }

Property Value

bool

Children 

public Element[] Children { get; init; }

Property Value

Element[]

ClipToBounds 

public bool ClipToBounds { get; init; }

Property Value

bool

Corners 

public CornerRadius4 Corners { get; init; }

Property Value

CornerRadius4

CounterScale

Opt this child OUT of a ScaleCorrect ancestor's scale: the recorder applies the inverse scale so the child stays undistorted (Framer-Motion projection correction).

public bool CounterScale { get; init; }

Property Value

bool

Cursor

Pointer cursor shown while hovering this node or any cursor-less descendant (WinUI SetCursor). Null = inherit from the nearest declaring ancestor, else the system arrow — clickability does NOT imply the hand. An explicit value (Arrow included) terminates the lookup, masking an ancestor's I-beam/hand (the TextBox delete button / PasswordBox reveal force Arrow over the field's I-beam — TextBox_Partial.cpp:884).

public CursorId? Cursor { get; init; }

Property Value

CursorId?

Direction 

public byte Direction { get; init; }

Property Value

byte

Draggable

E5-L2 typed drag SOURCE (the Flutter Draggable / react-beautiful-dnd model — deliberately NOT WinUI OLE, per the 2026-06-10 user ruling): marks this box draggable (implies CanDrag — the L1 gesture armer) with a string Kind discriminator + a payload factory the engine resolves ONCE when the press promotes past the drag box. The live DragSession then routes to the nearest accepting DropTarget under the pointer on every move.

public DragSource? Draggable { get; init; }

Property Value

DragSource

DropTarget

E5-L2 drop TARGET (Flutter DragTarget / SwiftUI dropDestination): accepts sessions whose Kind is in AcceptKinds — OnEnter/OnOver/OnLeave fire on hover transitions, OnDrop on release over it (before the L1 completion; SettleOnDrop keeps the drop-glide for reorder targets). Discovery is hit-test-CHAIN based (nearest accepting ancestor of the node under the pointer) — the spec alone does NOT make this box click/pointer hit-testable.

public DropTargetSpec? DropTarget { get; init; }

Property Value

DropTargetSpec

ElementTypeId

Stable per-record-type id for integer type-dispatch in the reconciler (the source-gen'd ElementTypeId).

public override ushort ElementTypeId { get; }

Property Value

ushort

Fill

Unified channel (Prop<T>): a static color, a Func<ColorF> thunk, or a concrete signal.

public Prop<ColorF> Fill { get; init; }

Property Value

Prop<ColorF>

FocusVisualMargin

WinUI FocusVisualMargin: negative values push the keyboard-focus ring OUTSIDE the bounds. Null = the WinUI template default (−3 all around); Slider uses −7,0,−7,0.

public Edges4? FocusVisualMargin { get; init; }

Property Value

Edges4?

HoverScale

Property Value

float

IsEnabled

Input-enabled (the default). When false the engine gates this node's interaction: it does not hit-test, focus, take keyboard activation, repeat, drag, or click — so control factories no longer null their handlers by hand. Disabled visuals stay control-chosen (pick the disabled token via StateBrush.Resting(enabled)).

public bool IsEnabled { get; init; }

Property Value

bool

Justify 

public FlexJustify Justify { get; init; }

Property Value

FlexJustify

float

MinWidth

float

MorphId

Optional shared-element key: a node with the same MorphId across reconciles morphs from the old node's rect to the new one (matched-geometry / Hero). Reserved for v1.1.

public string? MorphId { get; init; }

Property Value

string

public float OffsetX { get; init; }

Property Value

float

OffsetY 

public float OffsetY { get; init; }

Property Value

float

OnCharInput

Text (character) input — the IME/layout-resolved codepoint, routed to the focused node and bubbled (distinct from OnKeyDown's raw virtual-key). Set by editable controls (EditableText/ComboBox).

public Action<CharEventArgs>? OnCharInput { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<CharEventArgs>

OnClick 

public Action? OnClick { get; init; }

Property Value

Action

OnContextRequested

Context-menu request (WinUI ContextRequested): right-click release over this node, or the Menu key / Shift+F10 while it has focus. Local coords (keyboard invocations pass the node's centre).

public Action<Point2>? OnContextRequested { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<Point2>

OnDrag 

public Action<Point2>? OnDrag { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<Point2>

OnDragCanceled

The drag aborted (Escape / pointer-capture loss / window blur): drop hints without committing.

public Action? OnDragCanceled { get; init; }

Property Value

Action

OnDragCompleted

Release after an active drag (WinUI DragItemsCompleted): commit the reorder here (ReorderList.Complete()); the drop-glide and the displaced-sibling FLIP retarget off this commit.

public Action<DragEventArgs>? OnDragCompleted { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<DragEventArgs>

OnDragDelta

Every pointer move while the drag is active: accumulated gesture deltas + smoothed velocity — feed ReorderList.Update(e.TotalDy) and re-render with its projected order / offset hints.

public Action<DragEventArgs>? OnDragDelta { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<DragEventArgs>

OnDragStarted

Drag lifecycle (needs CanDrag): fired once when the press crosses the drag box (WinUI DragItemsStarting). The args instance is reused for the whole gesture — copy what you keep.

public Action<DragEventArgs>? OnDragStarted { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<DragEventArgs>

OnFocusChanged

Fired when the dispatcher moves keyboard/pointer focus ONTO (true) or OFF (false) this node — the WinUI GotFocus/LostFocus pair. Delivered by InputDispatcher.SetFocus directly (never via hit-testing); editable controls use it to arm the caret blinker / IME and capture the Escape-revert snapshot.

public Action<bool>? OnFocusChanged { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<bool>

OnHoverMove

Position-aware BARE hover (local coords), fired on pointer move while hovering with no button down — e.g. RatingControl filling stars to the cursor on hover. Makes the node hit-testable so it receives hover.

public Action<Point2>? OnHoverMove { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<Point2>

OnKeyDown 

public Action<KeyEventArgs>? OnKeyDown { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<KeyEventArgs>

OnPinned

Observable pin state for a StickyTop box — the signals-native CSS :stuck: the host invokes this whenever the box transitions pinned ↔ unpinned. Wire it to a signal and restyle ANYTHING off it (an opaque fill so content can't show through, a shadow, a compact variant, different children) — strictly more flexible than a fixed pinned style. Fired at most once per transition, never per frame.

public Action<bool>? OnPinned { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<bool>

OnPointerDown 

public Action<Point2>? OnPointerDown { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<Point2>

OnPointerExit

Fired when the pointer LEAVES this node (loses hover) — to reset a hover preview to its resting state (RatingControl reverting to the committed rating, a ToolTip dismissing). Makes the node hit-testable.

public Action? OnPointerExit { get; init; }

Property Value

Action

OnPointerPressed

Position-aware press carrying click count (double/triple-click), modifier chord, button and device kind — the text-selection / list-interaction press handler. Fires alongside OnPointerDown on left press.

public Action<PointerEventArgs>? OnPointerPressed { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<PointerEventArgs>

OnPointerWheel

Element-level wheel hook (WinUI PointerWheelChanged), consulted BEFORE the enclosing viewport scrolls; set Handled to consume (NumberBox value stepping). Unhandled keeps walking up (routed-event semantics).

public Action<WheelEventArgs>? OnPointerWheel { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<WheelEventArgs>

OnRealized

Called once when this box is realized into the scene, with its node handle — for a control factory to capture the handle (e.g. to wire a signal binding that needs the live node). Fires at mount only.

public Action<NodeHandle>? OnRealized { get; init; }

Property Value

Action<NodeHandle>

Opacity

Unified channel (Prop<T>): a static opacity, a thunk, or a concrete signal.

public Prop<float> Opacity { get; init; }

Property Value

Prop<float>

OpacityGroup

Flat opacity group (WinUI Composition LayerVisual semantics): when set and the resolved opacity is < 1, the subtree renders at FULL alpha into a pooled offscreen RT and composites ONCE at the group alpha — overlapping children don't double-blend (a fading dialog plate + its buttons). Default false = per-node multiplied opacity (WinUI's plain Visual.Opacity behavior). Engine primitive: PushLayer{Opacity} (E9).

public bool OpacityGroup { get; init; }

Property Value

bool

Edges4

PressedOpacity

Property Value

float

RepeatDelayMs

WinUI RepeatButton Delay/Interval (ms) for Repeats nodes. NaN = the WinUI DP defaults (500/33); the ScrollBar template arrows use Interval=50 (ScrollBar_themeresources.xaml).

public float RepeatDelayMs { get; init; }

Property Value

float

RepeatIntervalMs 

public float RepeatIntervalMs { get; init; }

Property Value

float

Repeats

Opt this clickable node into auto-repeat: while held, the host's RepeatTicker re-invokes OnClick after an initial delay, then at a fixed interval (WinUI RepeatButton). Pauses while the held pointer leaves the node (fresh delay on re-entry — RepeatButton_Partial.cpp:530-574); a held Space arms the same engine timer.

public bool Repeats { get; init; }

Property Value

bool

Role

Semantic control role (set by the control factories; a button IS a BoxEl). Surfaced to a11y/devtools/tests.

public AutomationRole Role { get; init; }

Property Value

AutomationRole

Rotation 

public float Rotation { get; init; }

Property Value

float

ScaleX 

public float ScaleX { get; init; }

Property Value

float

ScaleY 

public float ScaleY { get; init; }

Property Value

float

Shadow 

public ShadowSpec? Shadow { get; init; }

Property Value

ShadowSpec?

Shrink 

public float Shrink { get; init; }

Property Value

float

StickyTop

CSS position: sticky; top: N — the box scrolls normally until its top edge reaches N device- independent pixels below its nearest scroll viewport's top, then PINS there while its PARENT (the containing block) is in view; it never escapes the parent's bounds. The pin is a per-frame LocalTransform written by the host (compositor-only, no relayout) and hit-testing follows the pinned position; while pinned the node paints ABOVE its siblings so content scrolls underneath. One transform owner per node: do not combine with TransformBind or transform-channel animations. Null = not sticky (the default).

public float? StickyTop { get; init; }

Property Value

float?

TabIndex 

public int TabIndex { get; init; }

Property Value

int

TabStop

WinUI Control.IsTabStop: null = auto (clickable nodes are focusable), false = NEVER keyboard focusable even when clickable (the light-dismiss catcher layer — WinUI's dismiss layer is not a tab stop), true = force focusable.

public bool? TabStop { get; init; }

Property Value

bool?

Transform

The whole-matrix transform channel — bind-only in v1 (a thunk/signal producing the full Affine2D; the STATIC path composes from the decomposed OffsetX/Y/ScaleX/Y/Rotation floats above). When bound, the decomposed statics are ignored: one transform owner per node — never combine with StickyTop or transform-channel animations. An unbound Transform's Value is never read.

public Prop<Affine2D> Transform { get; init; }

Property Value

Prop<Affine2D>

TransformOriginX

Transform origin (normalized 0..1 of the box). Composited scale/rotate (and animated ScaleX/Y) pivot here; default centre (0.5,0.5). Set OriginY=0 to scale/unfold from the TOP edge (a flyout/menu), 1 for the bottom.

public float TransformOriginX { get; init; }

Property Value

float

TransformOriginY 

public float TransformOriginY { get; init; }

Property Value

float

Width

Unified channels (Prop<T>): static size, thunk, or concrete signal (bound ⇒ scoped relayout).

public Prop<float> Width { get; init; }

Property Value

Prop<float>

Wrap 

public bool Wrap { get; init; }

Property Value

bool

ZStack

Z-stack: children overlay at this box's origin (each filling it unless sized), painted in order (last on top) — for overlays, scrims, flyouts, the NavigationView Minimal pane. (A flexbox container otherwise.)

public bool ZStack { get; init; }

Property Value

bool
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
